name: Rutland (United Kingdom)
description: A green field with a central acorn-and-oak motif and a horseshoe — emblems of England's smallest historic county.
history: Adopted by Rutland County Council and registered with the Flag Institute in 2014. The acorn alludes to Rutland's woodland; the horseshoe to the medieval Oakham horseshoe tradition.

features.symbolism: the centuries-old Oakham Castle tradition by which visiting nobles forfeit a horseshoe
